item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like this SCP succeeded at establishing the tone 5104 attempted. (Note: I'm currently reading batches of SCPs in numerical order. I know 5104 and 5106 are not related.) The Foundation agents here are a bit fumbling, but their behavior is roughly within the bounds of plausibility; they underestimated their opponent, didn't take its cleverness seriously, and let their guard down.

This has some plausibility issues too (how did 5106 figure out how to deploy a sophisticated weapon designed specifically for human use that it had only discovered moments earlier, with no opportunity for practice or experimentation?), but all the same, I'm a bit sad to see 5104 has about three times as many upvotes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this was a great read. not to mention, this is totally something geese would do. i'm wondering if you were inspired by i am goose. if you were you made the goose theif and his posse a million times better. 10/10 honks

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gonna be honest kinda funny thid scp exists now while one of the canon contest teams is about geese

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know what? I dig crime goose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This went up to the line between mainlist and -J and danced the tarantella all over it.

5106 is a believable character; I really believed every interaction. The squad of henchgeese was an especially nice touch.

This is clearly a job for Dr. Spanko.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was pretty enjoyable. I was reminded of an episode of Regular show where a gang of geese takes over a park and starts wreaking havoc.

Great stinger at the end too. Easy +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a riot, and I am here for it.

item.title: H O N K
item.content:encoded: Wrote this awhile ago, decided to finally post it– get goosed.
